twitter_thread: My 5-stock portfolio generated $23k in lifetime passive income, with 3M yielding 4.1% and Coca-Cola at 3.2%.
ExxonMobil and Chevron pay 5% and 4.7% respectively, but I've seen 12.1% annual returns from Simon Property Group REIT.
Avoid portfolio overlap: my energy sector is capped at 20% to minimize risk.
Altria stock yields 7.1%, but tobacco sales are declining 3.5% YoY, so I'm cautious.
I use Dividend.com to track my portfolio's 8.5% average yield and optimize for tax efficiency.
Diversification works: my tech holdings like Intel and Johnson & Johnson provide a 6.2% average yield.
What's your highest-yielding dividend stock, and how do you manage risk? #passiveincome #dividends
pack.linkedin: I've generated $23k in lifetime passive income from a portfolio of 5 carefully selected dividend stocks, including 3M yielding 4.1% and Coca-Cola at 3.2%. By capping my energy sector at 20% and using tools like Dividend.com, I've achieved an 8.5% average yield. What strategies have you used to build a successful dividend portfolio, and what lessons have you learned from stocks like Altria, with its 7.1% yield but declining sales?
tiktok_hooks: Twenty-three grand from five stocks, no get-rich-quick scheme
Oil stocks pay 5%, but REITs like Simon Property Group pay 12%
Don't put more than 20% in one sector, or you'll get burned
pack.reddit_headline: Can you really earn $20k+ in lifetime passive income from just 5 dividend stocks, or is that just a myth?
